mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market signals an impending economic contraction, triggering corporate earnings revisions downward and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal fails to predict earnings downgrades within two quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in consumer credit extension and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative Rank IC over any 12-month rolling window, or forward returns do not significantly outperform when either proxy individually signals strength.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which lowers input cost pressures and supports equity valuations over a one-week forward horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain flat or negative during consecutive monthly INDPRO increases exceeding 1.5%, indicating the signal has lost predictive power for short-term equity movements.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands the corporate earnings base, which mechanically lifts equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon regardless of the prevailing monetary stance.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth surprises fail to correlate with 60-day forward SPX returns (correlation coefficient < 0.10), or earnings revisions trend downward despite positive GDP acceleration.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent consumer price inflation above the 330 index level forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Equity forward returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero when CPIAUCSL stays above 330 for three consecutive months, indicating markets have fully priced in the inflation risk.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong nonfarm payroll growth and a stable unemployment rate confirm a healthy labor market without overheating, which sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative information coefficient over any 6-month rolling window, or forward returns are not significantly higher when PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k but UNRATE simultaneously rises.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts combined with declining consumer credit indicates a demand shock that will reduce corporate revenues, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or statistically neutral when both HOUST and RSAFS decline simultaneously for two consecutive months, proving the demand shock hypothesis is invalid.
